item.title: The case for declaring an early victory in Iran
item.description: The temptation to dig in will grow. Trump can avoid it by declaring victory soon.

item.title: The dumbest way to lower beef prices
item.description: Breaking up meatpacking companies would make steak even more expensive.

item.title: The government’s freeze on nuclear energy is thawing
item.description: The NRC speedily approved a construction permit for TerraPower’s nuclear reactor.

item.title: An Iranian civil war is not in America’s interest
item.description: Israel may welcome the chaos of regime collapse. The United States and its allies cannot.

item.title: Cartoon by Lisa Benson
item.description: Lisa Benson cartoon on Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz and fraud hearing.

item.title: The real Ali Khamenei
item.description: The ayatollah’s “avuncular” style, ICE walkouts and AI jobs panic, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: Who’s more embarrassing — the U.S. men’s hockey team or their critics?
item.description: The state of the union and light pollution, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: Trump’s mini-me ambassadors are insulting and alienating U.S. allies
item.description: Save the undiplomatic diplomats. The president does enough damage to America’s reputation all by himself.

item.title: War-making is the president’s business, not Congress’s
item.description: The executive branch is too powerful, yes, but Trump was free to act against Iran.

item.title: The U.S. can’t afford to offshore clinical trials to China
item.description: A burdensome regulatory environment is pushing clinical trials overseas.

item.title: How China’s enormous bet on Iran failed
item.description: The Chinese Communist Party’s investment in Iran has been monumental.

item.title: Why America needs evangelicals on the Supreme Court — and more
item.description: The lack of evangelical Christians at America’s most prestigious institutions fuels mistrust.

item.title: This masterwork is turning 250. It’s needed now more than ever.
item.description: Adam Smith’s free-markets masterwork, published on March 9, 1776, was about much more than economics.

item.title: A better way to make homeownership more affordable
item.description: Congress can’t solve the housing shortage. The onus is on local governments.

item.title: Iran war sends gas prices climbing — what happens next?
item.description: An upcoming Washington Post Opinions show. Stay tuned.

item.title: Choosing Khamenei’s successor
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Iran moderating.

item.title: Kristi Noem’s ouster at DHS allows for a needed overhaul
item.description: Kristi Noem’s ouster at DHS allows for a needed overhaul.

item.title: In defense of sugar
item.description: Republicans used to be annoyed at government telling people what to eat.

item.title: The dangerous rise of decapitation warfare
item.description: Removing an enemy leader is easy compared with producing a stable political outcome.

item.title: Why it matters that the government is messing with health databases
item.description: There is no replacement for reliable data from federal government databases.
